name: Pita Breads
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 teaspoons active dry yeast (half of a ¼ oz packet)
about 3 ½ cups flour (you can use whole wheat for some of the flour)
recipeInstructions:
(from Mollie Katzen's 15th anniversary revised Moosewood Cookbook)
This recipe makes twelve small pitas or six large pitas. I don't double it because the pitas need to rest after being formed and I don't have that much available level space.
1. Sprinkle yeast into water and wait until foamy.
2. Add sugar or honey and salt, then stir to dissolve.
3. Beat in 1 cup flour for a smooth batter.
4. Gradually add the remaining flour to form a fairly soft dough.
5. Knead until smooth. Cover with a damp towel; let rise until doubled.
6. Punch down and knead a few minutes.
7. Divide into six or twelve pieces.
8. Knead each portion and roll into 1/8 - 1/4 inch thick round shapes. (Note: The shape and size don't matter as much as thickness.)
9. It's better to err on the side of too thick, as too thin will burn easily and become cracker-like.
10. Use plenty of flour when rolling. Let rounds rest at least 30 minutes covered with a damp towel (I rest them on placemats).
11. Preheat oven to 500°F and heat baking sheets. I use just one sheet and bake pitas two at a time.
12. Brush hot sheets with oil or sprinkle with cornmeal (or sesame seeds). Place the pitas on hot sheets and bake for 5-8 minutes until puffed like blowfish and slightly browned. Watch closely; they bake quickly.
13. Wrap pitas in damp towel and place them in a paper or plastic grocery bag for 15 minutes. This step keeps them supple.
